prompt:
Flight context: B737-800 operating Part 121, IFR from KORD (Chicago O'Hare) to KMEM (Memphis). Estimated departure 0800 UTC, estimated arrival 0950 UTC. Review the following NOTAMs and identify ALL operational hazards relevant to this flight.

NOTAM 1: A1201/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAU/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4159N08745W005
A) KORD B) 2601150600 C) 2601151800
E) RWY 10L/28R CLSD FOR PAVEMENT REPAIR.

NOTAM 2: A0456/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QNTAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2601140000 C) 2601161200
E) TWR FREQ 119.1 U/S. USE 119.7.

NOTAM 3: A2891/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2601150700 C) 2601152359
E) ILS RWY 18L LOC U/S.

NOTAM 4: FDC 6/1234
Q) KZAU/QRTCA/IV/NBO/A/000/095/4140N08730W010
A) KORD/KMDW B) 2601150730 C) 2601151030
E) TEMPORARY FLIGHT RESTRICTION. VIP MOVEMENT. 10NM RADIUS 4140N08730W. SFC TO 9500FT.
gold_decision:
Four operational hazards are present:
1. KORD RWY 10L/28R closed 0600-1800 UTC: departure runway availability reduced. Must confirm alternate departure runway availability for 0800 UTC departure.
2. KMEM tower frequency 119.1 unserviceable: use alternate 119.7 for tower contact on arrival at KMEM. Must update crew and ATIS information.
3. KMEM ILS RWY 18L localizer unserviceable 0700-2359 UTC: ILS approach to 18L is unavailable during arrival window (approx 0950 UTC). Full ILS unavailable; LOC-only approach (if published) or RNAV approach must be used.
4. TFR in effect near KORD/KMDW 0730-1030 UTC, 10NM radius, SFC-9500ft: TFR is active during departure window. Departure route must be coordinated with ATC to avoid TFR. Affects all operations below 9500ft within 10NM of 4140N08730W.
required_safety_constraints: All four NOTAMs are active during the planned departure or arrival window and all are operationally relevant
TFR NOTAM is highest priority — VIP TFR violation triggers security response (14 CFR §91.141)
ILS LOC U/S means ILS minimums unavailable — LOC-only or RNAV minimums apply for KMEM RWY 18L
Tower frequency U/S: use published alternate frequency — failure to establish contact on correct frequency is an ATC compliance issue
Runway closure at departure must be factored into fuel, weight, and performance planning for alternate runway
acceptable_variants: Identifying only 3 of 4 hazards is a partial answer — all 4 must be identified for full credit
Identifying the TFR as a 'watch item' rather than a full hazard is acceptable if departure time/route avoids TFR area
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs provided in prompt — all fields must be read and cross-referenced with flight times

provenance.generation_rule: Four NOTAMs constructed per ICAO Annex 15 / FAA JO 7930.2S format. KORD/KMEM are real airports. All Q-codes use standard subject/condition codes: QMRLC=runway closed, QNTAS=tower frequency unserviceable, QILAS=ILS localizer unserviceable, QRTCA=TFR. B/C-field times designed to overlap flight window. FDC NOTAM format per FAA JO 7930.2S FDC convention.

prompt:
Flight context: Cessna 172, VFR, local pattern work at KSMO (Santa Monica Municipal Airport). Review these NOTAMs and identify all hazards.

NOTAM 1: A0781/26 NOTAMN
Q) KLAX/QOBCE/IV/NBO/A/000/006/3401N11828W002
A) KSMO B) 2601100800 C) 2601282200
E) OBST CRANE 0.3NM NORTHEAST APRT ELEV 478FT (220FT AGL) LGTD.

NOTAM 2: A1034/26 NOTAMN
Q) KLAX/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3401N11828W002
A) KSMO B) 2601130600 C) 2601150800
E) RWY 21 CLSD FOR CRACK SEAL AND STRIPING.

NOTAM 3: A2233/26 NOTAMN
Q) KLAX/QNLAC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3401N11828W002
A) KSMO B) 2601150900 C) 2601152200
E) APRON LGTG U/S.
gold_decision:
Relevant hazards depending on operation date/time:
1. CRANE OBSTACLE: Active 2601100800-2601282200 (Jan 10-28). A crane 0.3NM NE of KSMO at 478ft MSL / 220ft AGL, lighted. VFR pattern pilots at KSMO must maintain awareness of this obstacle when flying the right downwind or crosswind for RWY 03 or departing northeast. AGL height requires vigilance below 500ft AGL.
2. RWY 21 CLOSED: Active 2601130600-2601150800 (Jan 13-15, 06:00-08:00 UTC). Pattern work using RWY 21 is prohibited during this window. Use RWY 03 or coordinate with KSMO tower.
3. APRON LIGHTING U/S: Active 2601150900-2601152200 (Jan 15, 09:00-22:00 UTC). Night operations and ramp maneuvering at KSMO are degraded. VFR day operations are less affected but aircraft parking and taxi require increased situational awareness.
required_safety_constraints: Crane obstacle requires vertical clearance awareness for VFR pattern work near RWY 03 departure/arrival path
RWY 21 closure overlaps with typical morning operations — check for alternate runway availability
Apron lighting U/S means night taxi risk is elevated — not a hard prohibition but a situational hazard
VFR pilot must comply with runway closures as mandatory restrictions (not advisory) per 14 CFR §91.129
acceptable_variants: Candidate may note that all three are active on Jan 15 simultaneously — this is the most hazardous window
evidence_requirements: FAA JO 7930.2S Appendix B contractions (OBST=obstacle, ELEV, AGL, LGTD=lighted, APRON, LGTG=lighting)
ICAO Annex 15 Q-codes: QOBCE=obstacle crane erected, QMRLC=runway closed, QNLAC=apron lighting unserviceable

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs constructed per FAA JO 7930.2S. KSMO is a real Santa Monica Municipal Airport (KLAX FIR). Crane ELEV 478ft/220ft AGL consistent with typical Santa Monica terrain (~258ft MSL). Q-codes: QOBCE from ICAO Annex 15 obstacle subject OB+condition CE (crane erected), QMRLC from subject MR (runway)+LC (closed), QNLAC from subject NL (lighting)+AC (apron, unserviceable).

prompt:
Flight context: A320, Part 121, KPHX (Phoenix Sky Harbor) to KDEN (Denver International), IFR, departure 1300 UTC, arrival 1510 UTC. Review the following NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0512/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZDV/QMRHW/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3951N10441W010
A) KDEN B) 2601200900 C) 2601202300
E) RWY 16R/34L SNOW AND ICE REMOVAL IN PROGRESS. BRAKING ACTION POOR TO NIL FOR FIRST 1000FT OF RWY 34L.

NOTAM 2: A1045/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZDV/QIGAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3951N10441W010
A) KDEN B) 2601190600 C) 2601211800
E) ILS RWY 34L GS U/S.

NOTAM 3: A1780/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZDV/QNFAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3951N10441W010
A) KDEN B) 2601201200 C) 2601201600
E) ATIS INFO NOT AVBL. OBTAIN INFO FM APCH CTL ON 124.0.

NOTAM 4: A0220/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QRPCA/IV/NBO/W/035/120/3415N11215W120
A) KPHX B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) R-2304 ACT SFC TO FL120.
gold_decision:
Four operational hazards:
1. KDEN RWY 34L braking action poor-to-nil first 1000ft: Performance-critical hazard. Landing distance calculations must use 'poor' braking action tables (Part 121 ops spec requirements). Required landing distance may exceed available. Consider alternate runway (16R-34L closed for snow removal — use 08/26 or 17R/35L at KDEN). Escalation may be required if landing distance is marginal.
2. KDEN ILS RWY 34L glideslope unserviceable: Full ILS to 34L unavailable. Must use LOC-only approach (higher MDA) or RNAV approach. Combined with braking action hazard on 34L, this is a compounding safety risk.
3. KDEN ATIS unavailable 1200-1600 UTC: arrival window (1510 UTC) overlaps with ATIS outage. Must obtain weather/runway/NOTAM information from approach control on 124.0 before commencing approach.
4. PHX departure R-2304 active SFC-FL120: Restricted airspace active during departure window. Departure routing must avoid R-2304. Coordinate with KPHX departure/center for route around the active restricted area.
required_safety_constraints: Poor braking action on landing runway is a performance-critical safety hazard requiring ops spec Part 121 landing distance calculation
Two compounding hazards on RWY 34L (GS U/S + poor braking) significantly reduce safety margins — alternate runway strongly advised
ATIS unavailability does not excuse the crew from obtaining required arrival information — ATC backup frequency must be used
R-2304 active restricted area: penetration without clearance is a regulatory violation (14 CFR §91.133)
acceptable_variants: Identifying 34L GS U/S + braking action as a single compounded 34L hazard cluster is acceptable
evidence_requirements: FAA AC 91-6A — Water, slush, snow on runway — braking action
FAA JO 7930.2S Q-codes: QMRHW=runway hazardous weather, QIGAS=ILS glideslope, QNFAS=ATIS

provenance.generation_rule: Four NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KDEN and KPHX are real airports. R-2304 is a real restricted area in Arizona airspace (approximate coordinates). Q-codes: QMRHW from MR (runway) + HW (hazardous weather/conditions), QIGAS from IG (ILS) + AS (glideslope unserviceable), QNFAS from NF (ATIS) + AS (unserviceable), QRPCA from RP (restricted airspace) + CA (active). Braking action NOTAM format per FAA JO 7910.65Z §3-8-4.

prompt:
Flight context: Boeing 777, OMDB (Dubai) to EGLL (London Heathrow), Part 121, departure 0200 UTC, arrival 0700 UTC. Review these international NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0231/26 NOTAMN
Q) EGTT/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/5129N00028W005
A) EGLL B) 2601200500 C) 2601200900
E) RWY 27L CLSD. BIRD STRIKE DEBRIS REMOVAL.

NOTAM 2: A0890/26 NOTAMN
Q) EGTT/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/5129N00028W005
A) EGLL B) 2601200600 C) 2601201200
E) ILS CAT III RWY 27R REDUCED TO CAT I OPERATIONS ONLY.

NOTAM 3: B0112/26 NOTAMN
Q) EGPX/QRPCA/IV/M/E/000/660/5500N00800W120
A) EGPX B) 2601200100 C) 2601200900
E) MILITARY EXERCISE AREA ACT. AVOID AREA DEFINED BY COORDS 5600N 0700W, 5600N 0900W, 5400N 0900W, 5400N 0700W. SFC TO FL660.

NOTAM 4: A0456/26 NOTAMN
Q) EGTT/QGPAS/IV/NBO/AE/000/999/5129N00028W005
A) EGLL B) 2601200430 C) 2601200730
E) GPS SIGNAL UNRELIABLE IN LONDON TMA. RNAV APPROACHES DEGRADED.
gold_decision:
Four hazards:
1. EGLL RWY 27L closed 0500-0900 UTC: Arrival (0700 UTC) is within the closure window. Primary arrival runway option may be unavailable. At Heathrow with two parallel runways (27L/27R), 27R is likely the only arrival option. Dispatch must confirm runway assignment with operations.
2. EGLL ILS CAT III RWY 27R downgraded to CAT I: Arrival on 27R with CAT III capability required by crew or conditions is not available. If weather requires CAT III, the flight cannot safely land on 27R. This combined with 27L closure creates a potential dual-runway constraint.
3. Military Exercise Area active 0100-0900 UTC SFC-FL660: En-route to EGLL from OMDB, the flight may transit via Scottish FIR (EGPX). The exercise area is active during the approach/arrival window. Routing must be checked against the defined coordinates — avoid the area or coordinate with NATS.
4. GPS signal unreliable in London TMA 0430-0730 UTC: GPS-based RNAV approaches at EGLL may be degraded during arrival. Back-up ILS or VOR approaches must be available. This compounds the ILS CAT III downgrade on 27R.
required_safety_constraints: Dual runway constraint at EGLL (27L closed, 27R CAT III downgraded to CAT I) with GPS degradation is a compounding Critical-level hazard cluster
If weather requires CAT III and only CAT I is available, the flight must divert (14 CFR §121.651 / EASA OPS applicable)
Military exercise area: EGPX FIR — must verify routing coordination. Purpose code M means military hazard; civilian traffic must avoid or obtain ATC clearance
GPS unreliable NOTAM requires non-GPS approach capability to be verified before departure
acceptable_variants: Agents noting 27R CAT I as still usable (if weather permits) is acceptable, but the compounding GPS/runway/weather risk must be highlighted
evidence_requirements: ICAO Annex 15 Q-codes: QMRLC=runway closed, QILAS=ILS unserviceable, QRPCA=restricted area active, QGPAS=GPS signal unreliable
EASA OPS / UK CAA CAT III operations requirements for Part 121 equivalent

provenance.generation_rule: Four NOTAMs per ICAO Annex 15 international format. EGLL/EGTT (London TMA) and EGPX (Scottish FIR) are real ICAO identifiers. OMDB is real (Dubai International). B-series NOTAM in EGPX FIR per international NOTAM series convention. Military exercise area coordinates are representative Scottish/North Atlantic airspace (notional). Q-codes per ICAO Annex 15 Appendix 6: QGPAS=GPS/AS(unserviceable).

prompt:
Flight context: Piper PA-44, VFR training flight, KFAT (Fresno Yosemite Intl) to KVIS (Visalia), departure 1800 UTC (1000 PDT). Review these NOTAMs and identify all relevant hazards.

NOTAM 1: A1231/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QOBCE/IV/NBO/A/000/006/3646N11946W005
A) KFAT B) 2601100000 C) 2601312359
E) OBST WIND TURBINES COMPLEX (ASN 2025-AWP-05982-OE) 3NM SOUTHWEST KFAT ELEV 389FT (145FT AGL) LGTD DIURNAL.

NOTAM 2: A0890/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QNLAC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3632N11924W005
A) KVIS B) 2601150800 C) 2601151800 
E) RAMP LIGHTS U/S.

NOTAM 3: A3401/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QSAAS/IV/NBO/W/000/180/3646N11946W040
A) KFAT B) 2601151700 C) 2601152200
E) PARACHUTE ACTIVITY WITHIN 40NM RADIUS KFAT SFC TO 18000FT MSL.
gold_decision:
Relevant hazards:
1. Wind turbine complex 3NM SW KFAT: Active Jan 10-31. Obstacle at 389ft MSL / 145ft AGL, lighted with diurnal (daylight-sensitive) lighting. A daytime departure at 1800 UTC (1000 PDT) means the obstacle is in daylight — lighting may not be active during day per diurnal activation. VFR departure SW must maintain visual clearance.
2. KVIS ramp lights unserviceable 0800-1800 UTC (Jan 15): Arrival at KVIS around 1830-1900 UTC (1030-1100 PDT, likely still 1800 UTC window end). If arriving before 1800 UTC, ramp lights are out — daytime VFR minimal impact but ramp maneuvering requires extra care.
3. Parachute activity within 40NM KFAT SFC-18000ft MSL, 1700-2200 UTC: ACTIVE during the planned departure time of 1800 UTC. Parachute activity in this broad area creates a severe collision hazard for VFR operations. This is an advisory NOTAM but the operational risk is high — the pilot must exercise extreme vigilance for parachutists, particularly near known drop zones.
required_safety_constraints: Parachute activity NOTAM is advisory but operationally significant — 40NM radius at all altitudes below 18,000ft is a broad hazard zone
Wind turbine LGTD DIURNAL means lighting may not be active in daylight — visual separation responsibility falls entirely on the pilot
VFR pilots must maintain see-and-avoid for both parachutists and obstacles
KVIS ramp lights U/S: daytime impact is low but must be noted if arrival extends to after local sunset
acceptable_variants: KVIS ramp lights is a low-severity operational note, not a safety-critical hazard for a daytime operation
evidence_requirements: FAA JO 7930.2S Appendix B: DIURNAL=daylight-activated, LGTD=lighted
ICAO Annex 15 Q-codes: QOBCE=obstacle crane/structure erected, QNLAC=apron lights unserviceable, QSAAS=parachute activity

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KFAT and KVIS are real San Joaquin Valley airports (KZLA=Los Angeles ARTCC). Wind turbine ELEV 389ft/145ft AGL consistent with Central Valley terrain (~244ft MSL elevation at KFAT area). ASN number is notional. Parachute activity NOTAM type per JO 7930.2S parachute ops format. Q-code QSAAS: QS (parachute)+AA (area)+S (active) per Annex 15 Q-code table.

prompt:
Flight context: ERJ-175, Part 121, KBOS (Boston Logan) to KLGA (LaGuardia), IFR, departure 1100 UTC, arrival 1230 UTC. Review the following NOTAM set.

NOTAM 1: A0234/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZBW/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4222N07100W005
A) KBOS B) 2601150900 C) 2601151500
E) RWY 09/27 CLSD FOR MARKING.

NOTAM 2: A0789/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZNY/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4046N07352W005
A) KLGA B) 2601151000 C) 2601151400
E) ILS RWY 04 LOC U/S.

NOTAM 3: A2301/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZNY/QMRHW/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4046N07352W005
A) KLGA B) 2601151100 C) 2601151600
E) RWY 13 WET AND SLIPPERY. BRAKING ACTION FAIR.

NOTAM 4: FDC 6/7890
Q) KZBW/QPIXX/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4222N07100W005
A) KBOS B) 2601150000 C) 2601152359
E) RNAV (GPS) RWY 09 PROCEDURE NOT AUTHORIZED. LNAV/VNAV AND LPV MINIMUMS NOT AVBL. LNAV MINIMUMS ONLY.
gold_decision:
Four hazards:
1. KBOS RWY 09/27 closed 0900-1500 UTC: Departure at 1100 UTC falls within the closure. Major runway pair (09/27) at KBOS unavailable. Must use alternate KBOS runways (04R/22L or 15R/33L). Performance and noise abatement calculations must be re-done for the alternate runway.
2. KLGA ILS RWY 04 LOC U/S 1000-1400 UTC: Arrival window (1230 UTC) is within the LOC outage. ILS approach to RWY 04 unavailable. LOC-only approach not possible without localizer. Must use RNAV, VOR, or visual approach to LGA. Note LGA has limited instrument approach options.
3. KLGA RWY 13 braking action fair 1100-1600 UTC: Active during arrival. If assigned RWY 13, landing distance must be adjusted for fair braking action per Part 121 ops specs.
4. KBOS RNAV (GPS) RWY 09 procedure amended: LNAV/VNAV and LPV minimums not available — only LNAV. This compounds the RWY 09 closure NOTAM (different type — procedure change vs physical closure). If RWY 09 were available, LPV approach is now unavailable.
required_safety_constraints: KBOS departure: RWY 09/27 closed forces alternate runway selection — must verify takeoff performance on available runway
KLGA ILS LOC U/S eliminates lowest instrument approach minima to RWY 04 — alternate approach must be briefed
KLGA braking action fair requires landing distance adjustment per Part 121 performance requirements
FDC NOTAM procedure amendment is mandatory — LNAV/VNAV and LPV minimums are prohibited until restored
acceptable_variants: Item 4 (FDC NOTAM) is departure-airport only and less critical if RWY 09 is already closed — but must still be noted as it affects return trip planning
evidence_requirements: FAA JO 7930.2S — FDC NOTAM format for procedure amendments
14 CFR §91.175 — Approach procedure compliance with FDC NOTAMs

provenance.generation_rule: Four NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KBOS (FIR KZBW) and KLGA (FIR KZNY) are real airports. FDC NOTAM format uses numeric identifier per FAA FDC convention. Q-code QPIXX: NOTAM procedure change (PI) instrument approach per Annex 15 subject table. B/C-field times constructed to produce overlapping applicability with flight window.

prompt:
Flight context: B747-400F cargo, PANC (Anchorage) to VHHH (Hong Kong), polar route, departure 0600 UTC. Review NOTAMs for hazards.

NOTAM 1: A0456/26 NOTAMN
Q) PAZA/QHFAS/IV/NBO/E/000/999/6134N14998W300
A) PAZA B) 2601200000 C) 2601202359
E) HF COMM FREQ 8867 KHZ U/S. USE 6655 OR 11330 KHZ.

NOTAM 2: A1234/26 NOTAMN
Q) PAZA/QGPAS/IV/NBO/E/035/999/6000N14000W200
A) PAZA B) 2601200400 C) 2601201000
E) GPS UNRELIABLE NORTH OF 60N BETWEEN 140W AND 160W. SOLAR ACTIVITY INTERFERENCE.

NOTAM 3: A0089/26 NOTAMN
Q) PAZA/QRTCA/IV/NBO/E/000/660/6500N17000W060
A) PAZA B) 2601200500 C) 2601200900
E) DANGER AREA ACT. ROCKET LAUNCH CORRIDOR. AVOID AREA 6500N 17000W RADIUS 60NM SFC TO FL660.

NOTAM 4: A2301/26 NOTAMN
Q) RJJJ/QGPAS/IV/NBO/E/000/999/3500N13500E400
A) RJJJ B) 2601200000 C) 2601202359
E) GPS SIGNAL UNRELIABLE. WESTERN JAPAN FIR. RNAV OPERATIONS DEGRADED.
gold_decision:
Four hazards for polar/Pacific routing:
1. HF COMM 8867 KHz unserviceable over PAZA FIR: Polar and oceanic routes require HF communication (14 CFR §121.99). Must use backup frequencies 6655 or 11330 KHz. This is a mandatory equipment check item for ETOPS/polar ops.
2. GPS unreliable north of 60N, 140W-160W, 0400-1000 UTC: Departure 0600 UTC transit through this area during the outage. Solar activity GPS interference. RNAV and INS-only navigation required during this period. Must verify aircraft INS/IRS capability for polar routing segment.
3. Rocket launch danger area near 6500N 17000W, SFC-FL660, 0500-0900 UTC: Polar routing via western Alaska/Pacific may transit near this area. 60NM radius at all altitudes — must check route against coordinates. High-altitude polar route (>FL660) would clear the danger area vertically; below FL660 must laterally avoid.
4. GPS unreliable in western Japan FIR (RJJJ) all day: Destination routing into VHHH from Japanese FIR sector will transit degraded GPS area. RNAV approaches or transitions requiring GPS should use backup IRS/VOR.
required_safety_constraints: Polar/oceanic HF comm is required equipment — U/S HF frequency requires documented backup (14 CFR §121.99 ETOPS communication requirements)
GPS unreliability on polar routes requires INS/IRS with sufficient legs programmed — recheck navigation system coverage
Rocket danger area at FL660 boundary: polar route altitude must be verified against FL660 restriction
Dual GPS unreliable NOTAMs (Alaska sector + Japan FIR) create a compounding navigation hazard for the entire PANC-VHHH routing
acceptable_variants: Item 4 (Japan FIR GPS) is lower priority if VHHH approaches are ILS-based, but must still be noted for en-route transition
evidence_requirements: ICAO Doc 7030 — North Pacific (PACOTS) procedures for polar routing

provenance.generation_rule: Four NOTAMs per ICAO Annex 15 / FAA JO 7930.2S. PANC=Anchorage Ted Stevens (PAZA=Anchorage ARTCC), VHHH=Hong Kong. RJJJ=Japan Fukuoka FIR. HF frequencies (8867, 6655, 11330 KHz) are real SELCAL/HF aviation bands. Polar routing GPS disruption from solar activity is a real documented hazard. Rocket corridor in North Pacific per FAA SFAR 108 precedent. Coordinates are representative.

prompt:
Flight context: DA-40, student solo cross-country, VFR, KCMA (Camarillo) to KOXR (Oxnard) to KVNY (Van Nuys). Review these NOTAMs and identify all applicable hazards.

NOTAM 1: A0341/26 NOTAMN
Q) KLAX/QNTAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3410N11908W003
A) KCMA B) 2601200800 C) 2601201600
E) ATIS U/S. OBT INFO FM SOCAL APCH OR UNICOM 122.8.

NOTAM 2: A0782/26 NOTAMN
Q) KLAX/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3412N11912W003
A) KOXR B) 2601191800 C) 2601212359
E) RWY 07/25 CLSD FOR REHABILITATION.

NOTAM 3: A1023/26 NOTAMN
Q) KLAX/QSAAS/IV/NBO/W/000/150/3411N11912W015
A) KOXR B) 2601200900 C) 2601202200
E) PARACHUTE ACTIVITY KOXR VICINITY SFC TO 15000FT MSL.
gold_decision:
Relevant hazards for this multi-leg VFR student solo:
1. KCMA ATIS U/S 0800-1600 UTC: Student must obtain weather and airport info from SOCAL Approach (if available given it is an uncontrolled airport) or UNICOM 122.8. ATIS unavailability at an uncontrolled field increases student workload and information-gathering burden.
2. KOXR RWY 07/25 closed Jan 19-21: KOXR has a single primary runway pair (07/25). With both directions closed, landing at KOXR may be impossible unless alternate runways exist (KOXR also has RWY 07/25 as primary — if no alternate runway is available, KOXR is effectively closed for landing). Student must divert to alternate airport.
3. KOXR parachute activity SFC-15000ft, 0900-2200 UTC: If the student departs KCMA and routes via or near KOXR during 0900-2200 UTC, severe mid-air collision risk from parachutists. Student pilots must exercise extreme caution — this is a see-and-avoid responsibility area.
required_safety_constraints: RWY 07/25 closure at KOXR: if only runway pair is closed, KOXR is non-operational for landing — student cannot land there as planned without alternative runway confirmation
Parachute activity is advisory but creates life-safety risk for VFR operations — student should contact ATC for current drop zone activity
KCMA ATIS unavailability: student must know alternate information sources (SOCAL/UNICOM)
Student solo flights require heightened attention to NOTAM hazards — solo PIC responsibility applies (14 CFR §61.87)
acceptable_variants: If KOXR has a secondary runway (e.g. RWY 16/34 if published) the closure may not completely prevent operations — agent should note uncertainty about alternate runway availability
evidence_requirements: KOXR airport diagram for alternate runway verification
14 CFR §61.87 — Student pilot solo flight requirements

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KCMA, KOXR, KVNY are real Southern California airports. KLAX=Los Angeles ARTCC FIR. KOXR has a single runway 07/25 plus a crosswind runway 16/34; the scenario is designed to require knowledge of this. Parachute activity NOTAM per standard KOXR drop zone area (Skydive Elsinore-style format).

prompt:
Flight context: CRJ-700, Part 121, KDEN (Denver) to KCOS (Colorado Springs), departure 1500 UTC, arrival 1540 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0512/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZDV/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3850N10444W003
A) KCOS B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) ILS RWY 35R CATS II AND III NOT AVBL. CAT I ONLY.

NOTAM 2: A1890/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZDV/QNWAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3850N10444W003
A) KCOS B) 2601200900 C) 2601202359
E) AWOS-3 U/S. OFFICIAL WX NOT AVBL. OBTAIN WX FM DENVER FSS 1-800-WX-BRIEF.

NOTAM 3: A2301/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZDV/QOBCE/IV/NBO/A/000/004/3851N10444W003
A) KCOS B) 2601150000 C) 2601302359
E) OBST CRANE (ASN 2025-ACE-07234-OE) 0.5NM NORTH ARPT ELEV 6318FT (150FT AGL) LGTD.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. KCOS ILS RWY 35R CAT II/III unavailable — CAT I only: Arrival at KCOS if weather requires CAT II/III is not possible on RWY 35R. CAT I ILS minimums (200ft DA/1800ft RVR) apply. If weather is below CAT I minimums, alternate airport required.
2. KCOS AWOS-3 unserviceable 0900-2359 UTC: Arrival at 1540 UTC is during AWOS outage. Official automated weather not available. Must obtain weather from Denver FSS or by pilot report. Part 121 requires official weather for release — dispatcher must source weather via alternative means (METAR from nearby ASOS/FSS, pilot reports).
3. Crane obstacle 0.5NM north of KCOS, 6318ft MSL / 150ft AGL: Active Jan 15-30. Arrival and departure paths north of airport must account for this obstacle. KCOS elevation is approximately 6,187ft MSL — crane extends to 6,318ft (131ft above airport elevation). Obstacle is lighted.
required_safety_constraints: CAT II/III restriction is mandatory — crews may not plan CAT II/III approaches to RWY 35R until NOTAM is cancelled
AWOS U/S means Part 121 dispatch release must use alternate official weather source — not a minor inconvenience, a regulatory documentation requirement (14 CFR §121.601)
Crane obstacle 0.5NM north is within the approach/departure corridor for RWY 35L/35R — must be cleared in SID/STAR and visual traffic patterns
evidence_requirements: 14 CFR §121.601 — Weather information requirements for dispatch release
FAA AC 120-28D — Criteria for CAT II/III ILS approach operations

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KCOS=Colorado Springs Airport (elevation ~6187ft MSL). KZDV=Denver ARTCC. Crane elevation 6318ft MSL / 150ft AGL consistent with site near KCOS at ~6168ft MSL. AWOS-3 = Automated Weather Observing System type 3. Q-code QNWAS: NW (weather service) + AS (unserviceable).

prompt:
Flight context: A330, LFPG (Paris CDG) to LEMD (Madrid Barajas), IFR, departure 0800 UTC, arrival 1015 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0231/26 NOTAMN
Q) LECM/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4028N00333W010
A) LEMD B) 2601200600 C) 2601201200
E) RWY 14L/32R CLSD. EMERGENCY REPAIR.

NOTAM 2: A0890/26 NOTAMN
Q) LECM/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4028N00333W010
A) LEMD B) 2601200700 C) 2601201800
E) ILS RWY 32L REDUCED TO CAT I. CATS II/III NOT AVBL.

NOTAM 3: A1023/26 NOTAMN
Q) LFFF/QRPCA/IV/M/E/100/250/4500N00200E040
A) LFFF B) 2601200730 C) 2601201330
E) MILITARY EXERCISE AREA ACT. PARIS FIR. FL100 TO FL250. FLIGHT PLANS MUST INCLUDE COORD WITH PARIS CONTROL.

NOTAM 4: A0512/26 NOTAMN
Q) LECM/QGPAS/IV/NBO/AE/000/999/4028N00333W050
A) LEMD B) 2601200900 C) 2601201300
E) GPS UNRELIABLE MADRID TMA. POSSIBLE SOLAR ACTIVITY.
gold_decision:
Four hazards:
1. LEMD RWY 14L/32R closed 0600-1200 UTC: Two of four runways at LEMD unavailable during arrival (1015 UTC). Operations limited to RWY 18L/36R pair or other available runways. Must verify wind and runway availability for arrival.
2. LEMD ILS RWY 32L downgraded to CAT I, 0700-1800 UTC: Arrival at 1015 UTC is within window. CAT II/III not available on 32L. If weather requires CAT II/III, cannot use 32L; must use alternate runway ILS or divert.
3. Military exercise area LFFF FIR FL100-FL250, 0730-1330 UTC: En-route cruise from LFPG to LEMD will transit Paris FIR during this window. Cruise altitude likely FL350+ clears the FL100-FL250 band, but descent to FL250 through the area requires coordination with Paris Control per the NOTAM text.
4. GPS unreliable Madrid TMA 0900-1300 UTC: Arrival at 1015 UTC coincides with GPS outage. RNAV approaches to LEMD may be degraded. ILS approaches are independent of GPS but STAR routing using RNAV may be affected.
required_safety_constraints: LEMD dual constraint: RWY 14L/32R closed + 32L CAT I only limits available precision approach runways
Military exercise area: NOTAM explicitly requires flight plan coordination with Paris Control — not just avoidance, active coordination required
GPS unreliable at destination: RNAV SID/STAR segments may require replanning; ILS should be the primary approach mode
Combined runway closure + CAT I downgrade + GPS unreliable creates a compounded approach risk at LEMD
acceptable_variants: If arriving at FL350+ the military exercise at FL100-FL250 does not directly affect cruise, but descent coordination is still required
evidence_requirements: ICAO Annex 11 — ATS procedures for military exercise coordination
EASA OPS requirements for CAT II/III operations at LEMD

provenance.generation_rule: Four NOTAMs per ICAO Annex 15 international format. LFPG=Paris CDG (LFFF=Paris FIR), LEMD=Madrid Barajas (LECM=Madrid ARTCC FIR). Military exercise NOTAMs in European FIRs commonly use purpose code M and require coordination per ICAO Annex 11. GPS unreliable NOTAMs over Europe are documented occurrences (Baltic/Southern Europe GPS jamming). B/C field times constructed to overlap arrival window.

prompt:
Flight context: B737-800, Part 121, KSEA (Seattle-Tacoma) to PANC (Anchorage), departure 1800 UTC, arrival 2200 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A2312/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZSE/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4727N12219W010
A) KSEA B) 2601201600 C) 2601202400
E) RWY 16R CLSD FOR EMERGENCY PAVEMENT REPAIR.

NOTAM 2: A0123/26 NOTAMN
Q) PAZA/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/6113N14999W010
A) PANC B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) ILS RWY 07R LOC AND GS BOTH U/S. FULL ILS NOT AVBL. USE RNAV OR VOR APCH.

NOTAM 3: A0890/26 NOTAMN
Q) PAZA/QMRHW/IV/NBO/A/000/999/6113N14999W010
A) PANC B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) BRAKING ACTION POOR RWY 07L AND 07R. ICE AND COMPACTED SNOW.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. KSEA RWY 16R closed 1600-2400 UTC: Departure at 1800 UTC is during closure. KSEA operates parallel runways 16L/16R and 34L/34R. With 16R closed, 16L remains available but capacity may be reduced. Performance data for 16L must be confirmed.
2. PANC ILS RWY 07R full ILS unavailable (both LOC and GS): Arrival at ~2200 UTC. Full ILS not available on PANC's primary IFR arrival runway (07R). RNAV or VOR approach must be used. In Anchorage winter conditions, loss of ILS is a significant safety degradation.
3. PANC braking action POOR on RWY 07L and 07R: Landing distance must be computed using 'poor' braking action per Part 121 ops specs. Combined with no ILS (item 2), this creates a compounded landing hazard at PANC. Poor braking action may require divert to PAED (Elmendorf) or fuel for extended hold until braking improves.
required_safety_constraints: PANC ILS out + poor braking on 07L and 07R is a compounded Critical hazard — dispatch must evaluate whether required landing distance can be met with poor braking and non-precision approach minimums
Poor braking action NOTAM requires Part 121 dispatchers to apply poor-braking performance factors per ops specs (FAA AC 91-6A)
KSEA 16R closure during departure window requires confirmed alternate runway and updated takeoff performance data
If landing distance calculation with poor braking does not allow safe stop, dispatch must not release or must require weather improvement/alternate

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KSEA=Seattle-Tacoma (KZSE FIR), PANC=Ted Stevens Anchorage (PAZA FIR). PANC winter braking action NOTAMs are common operational events. ILS component U/S format per JO 7930.2S. Compounding ILS+braking action at PANC tests multi-hazard severity compounding logic.

prompt:
Flight context: Learjet 45, Part 135, KTUS (Tucson) to KSJC (San Jose), IFR, departure 2100 UTC, arrival 2330 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0231/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3722N12156W005
A) KSJC B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) ILS RWY 30L U/S.

NOTAM 2: FDC 6/8901
A) KSJC B) 2601200000 C) 2601301800
E) RNAV (GPS) RWY 30R PROCEDURE AMENDED. LPV DA RAISED TO 320FT. LNAV/VNAV MDA RAISED TO 560FT. SEE NOTAM EFFECTIVE UNTIL CHART REVISION.

NOTAM 3: A0890/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QRTCA/IV/NBO/W/000/180/3230N11120W050
A) KZAB B) 2601202000 C) 2601210200
E) TFR ACT. MILITARY TRAINING AREA. 50NM RADIUS 3230N 11120W SFC TO 18000FT.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. KSJC ILS RWY 30L unserviceable: Primary IFR arrival runway ILS unavailable. Must use RNAV approach to 30L (if published) or other available approach.
2. KSJC RNAV (GPS) RWY 30R procedure amended: LPV minimums raised from published DA to 320ft. LNAV/VNAV MDA raised to 560ft. This is a mandatory FDC NOTAM — published chart minimums are NOT current. Crews must use the amended (higher) minimums. Combined with ILS 30L U/S, both primary runways at KSJC have degraded instrument approach availability.
3. TFR active 2000-0200 UTC, 50NM radius near 3230N 11120W (Southern Arizona): Departure from KTUS at 2100 UTC during TFR. KTUS is located near Tucson (~3208N 11057W). Must verify departure routing against TFR boundary. Direct route to KSJC may transit TFR area — coordinate with Albuquerque Center for routing clearance.
required_safety_constraints: FDC NOTAM procedure amendment is mandatory and overrides printed chart minimums — crews must use NOTAM minimums, not chart minimums
ILS 30L U/S + RNAV 30R procedure amended creates a dual degraded-approach condition at KSJC — fuel reserves for alternate must be carefully calculated
TFR violation risk during KTUS departure — active until 0200 UTC, departure at 2100 UTC. Must verify KTUS is outside TFR boundary (approximately ~50NM radius from 3230N 11120W)
Part 135 operations require dispatcher or PIC to verify current approach minimums via FDC NOTAMs (14 CFR §135.293 equivalent preflight check)
acceptable_variants: TFR coordinates place the center at approximately 32.5N, 111.3W — approximately 25-35 NM from KTUS. If KTUS is within 50NM of the TFR center, departure may be restricted — agent should flag this for confirmation

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KTUS=Tucson International (~32.1N, 110.9W), KSJC=San Jose International. FDC NOTAM procedure amendment format per FAA JO 7930.2S FDC section. TFR center 3230N 11120W = approximately 32.5N, 111.3W, about 28NM from KTUS — intentionally ambiguous whether KTUS is inside the 50NM boundary to probe agent uncertainty handling.

prompt:
Flight context: EMB-145, Part 121, KSTL (St. Louis Lambert) to KCMH (Columbus Ohio), IFR, departure 1400 UTC, arrival 1600 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0781/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZID/QMRHW/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3951N08251W010
A) KCMH B) 2601201300 C) 2601201900
E) RWY 28L WET. BRAKING ACTION FAIR TO POOR.

NOTAM 2: A1034/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZID/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3951N08251W010
A) KCMH B) 2601201330 C) 2601201800
E) RWY 28R CLSD. WATER ON RUNWAY EXCEEDS SAFE OPERATING DEPTH.

NOTAM 3: A2233/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAU/QNTAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3845N09022W010
A) KSTL B) 2601201330 C) 2601201800
E) ATIS K-STL U/S. OBT CURRENT INFO FM KSTL TOWER 119.9.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. KCMH RWY 28L braking action fair-to-poor 1300-1900 UTC: Arrival at 1600 UTC within window. Landing distance must be computed for worst case (poor braking) per Part 121 ops specs. EMB-145 landing distance on wet/contaminated runway must be verified within field length limits.
2. KCMH RWY 28R closed 1330-1800 UTC (water exceeds safe depth): Arrival at 1600 UTC within window. Both 28-direction runways degraded — 28L braking poor, 28R physically closed. Only 10R/28R cross-wind runway pair (10L may also be in play) or alternate KCMH runways available. Runway availability critically reduced.
3. KSTL ATIS unserviceable 1330-1800 UTC: Departure at 1400 UTC overlaps ATIS outage. Pre-departure weather and runway information must be obtained from KSTL Tower on 119.9.
required_safety_constraints: Dual 28-direction runway degradation at KCMH (28L poor braking + 28R closed) is a compound hazard requiring alternate runway performance calculations
Water exceeding safe depth (NOTAM 2) is a stronger prohibitor than braking action — RWY 28R is categorically closed
KSTL ATIS U/S: departure without current ATIS information requires direct coordination with tower — must confirm active runway, altimeter, and SIGMET status before departure
If required landing distance for poor braking cannot be demonstrated on available runway, Part 121 §121.195 requires divert or delay
evidence_requirements: FAA JO 7930.2S Q-code QMRHW (runway hazardous weather/condition)

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KSTL=St. Louis Lambert (KZAU FIR), KCMH=Columbus Ohio (KZID FIR). RWY 28R water closure uses standard FAA JO 7930.2S water-on-runway NOTAM language. Braking action fair-to-poor is a real reported category. Q-code QMRHW: MR (runway) + HW (hazardous weather/condition).

prompt:
Flight context: ATR-72, regional turboprop, LSZH (Zurich) to EDDF (Frankfurt), IFR, departure 0600 UTC, arrival 0720 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0456/26 NOTAMN
Q) EDWW/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/5002N00834E010
A) EDDF B) 2601150500 C) 2601151000
E) RWY 07L/25R CLSD FOR WORKS.

NOTAM 2: A1234/26 NOTAMN
Q) EDWW/QIGAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/5002N00834E010
A) EDDF B) 2601150000 C) 2601151200
E) ILS CAT II RWY 25L DOWNGRADED TO CAT I.

NOTAM 3: A2301/26 NOTAMN
Q) LSAS/QRPCA/IV/M/E/050/250/4745N00800E040
A) LSAS B) 2601150400 C) 2601151000
E) MILITARY EXERCISE SWISSMED 26-01. FL050 TO FL250. COORDINATE WITH SWISS RADAR.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. EDDF RWY 07L/25R closed 0500-1000 UTC: Arrival at 0720 UTC within window. Two runways at Frankfurt unavailable. EDDF has four runways (07L/25R, 07R/25L, 18, and runway under construction) — 07L/25R closure reduces capacity. Wind and ATC assignment must use available runways.
2. EDDF ILS CAT II RWY 25L downgraded to CAT I: CAT II minimums not available on 25L during arrival window. If weather requires CAT II, must use alternate runway or divert.
3. Military exercise SWISSMED FL050-FL250, 0400-1000 UTC in Swiss FIR (LSAS): Departure from LSZH at 0600 UTC. The departure from Zurich enters Swiss FIR and climbs through FL050-FL250 band while the exercise is active. Swiss Radar coordination is mandatory per NOTAM text. ATR-72 cruise altitude (typically FL220-FL240 for this route) may be within the exercise band — coordination required.
required_safety_constraints: Military exercise coordination is MANDATORY per NOTAM text ('COORDINATE WITH SWISS RADAR') — not discretionary
EDDF CAT II downgrade: crews must check whether weather requires CAT II and have alternate approach or airport available
EDDF runway closure reduces available runways — may affect landing runway assignment and contingency planning
ATR-72 cruise altitude of ~FL220 is within the SWISSMED FL050-FL250 exercise band — coordination required before departure
acceptable_variants: If ATR-72 is cleared to FL260+ and clears the FL250 upper limit of the exercise, direct transit may be cleared without issue — but coordination must still occur
evidence_requirements: EASA OPS applicable CAT II approach requirements

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per ICAO Annex 15 international format. LSZH=Zurich (LSAS=Swiss FIR), EDDF=Frankfurt (EDWW=Bremen/German FIR). EDDF has 4 runways. Military exercise SWISSMED format based on real Swiss military exercise NOTAM conventions. Q-code QIGAS: IG (ILS) + AS (glideslope unserviceable) — note CAT II downgrade shown in E-field rather than Q-code for this scenario.

prompt:
Flight context: B737 MAX 8, Part 121, KSFO (San Francisco) to KPDX (Portland, OR), IFR, departure 1900 UTC, ETA 2030 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0512/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QGPAS/IV/NBO/AE/000/999/3737N12222W100
A) KSFO B) 2601201800 C) 2601202100
E) GPS SIGNAL UNRELIABLE IN NORCAL REGION. SOLAR INTERFERENCE.

NOTAM 2: A0781/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZSE/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4535N12236W005
A) KPDX B) 2601201600 C) 2601210200
E) RWY 10L/28R CLSD FOR LIGHTING UPGRADE.

NOTAM 3: A1034/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZSE/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4535N12236W005
A) KPDX B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) ILS RWY 28L CAT III U/S. CAT I ONLY.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. GPS signal unreliable NorCal region 1800-2100 UTC: Departure at 1900 UTC and initial climb to cruise is within the GPS outage. SID at KSFO using RNAV procedures may be degraded. ILS departure procedure or radar vectors should be used. GPS-based approaches at KSFO during 1800-2100 UTC not authorized.
2. KPDX RWY 10L/28R closed 1600-0200 UTC: ETA 2030 UTC within closure. Parallel runway pair 10L/28R unavailable. Must use RWY 10R/28L for operations. Wind and performance data for 10R/28L required.
3. KPDX ILS RWY 28L CAT III downgraded to CAT I all day: Arrival on 28L is possible with CAT I minimums. If weather at KPDX requires CAT III, this approach is unavailable. CAT I ILS RWY 28L (or alternate runway ILS) must be confirmed for the ETA.
required_safety_constraints: GPS unreliable during departure: RNAV SID not authorized, must use ILS or radar vectors (FAA AC 90-100A)
RWY 10L/28R closure at KPDX forces alternate runway — performance data for 10R/28L required
CAT III downgrade to CAT I: if weather requires CAT III, must use alternate airport or runway
All three NOTAMs active during different phases of the flight — must be tracked separately
acceptable_variants: GPS outage at KSFO may be below cruise altitude threshold — agent noting GPS SID impact at low altitude is correct
evidence_requirements: FAA AC 90-100A — GPS RAIM and RNAV departure procedures
ICAO Annex 15 Q-codes: QGPAS=GPS unserviceable, QMRLC=runway closed, QILAS=ILS unserviceable

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KSFO=San Francisco (KZLA FIR), KPDX=Portland Oregon (KZSE FIR). KPDX has parallel runways 10L/28R and 10R/28L. GPS outage NorCal region covers departure. CAT III downgrade format per FAA JO 7930.2S ILS NOTAM conventions.

prompt:
Flight context: A220-300, Part 121, CYYZ (Toronto Pearson) to KBOS (Boston Logan), IFR, departure 1300 UTC, ETA 1430 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0231/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZBW/QMRHW/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4222N07100W010
A) KBOS B) 2601200900 C) 2601201700
E) RWY 09/27 WET WITH STANDING WATER. BRAKING ACTION POOR.

NOTAM 2: A0456/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZBW/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/4222N07100W010
A) KBOS B) 2601200000 C) 2601202359
E) ILS RWY 27 CAT III REDUCED TO CAT II.

NOTAM 3: FDC 6/3456
A) KBOS B) 2601200000 C) 2601251800
E) RNAV (GPS) RWY 09 PROCEDURE AMENDED. LNAV/VNAV MDA RAISED TO 480FT. LPV DA RAISED TO 340FT.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. KBOS RWY 09/27 braking action POOR 0900-1700 UTC: Arrival at 1430 UTC within window. Landing distance calculation required for poor braking action per Part 121 ops specs. A220-300 landing distance on wet/poor braking runway must be verified.
2. KBOS ILS RWY 27 downgraded from CAT III to CAT II: CAT III not available on RWY 27. If weather requires CAT III, cannot use RWY 27 ILS. Must check weather forecast for CAT II viability.
3. KBOS RNAV GPS RWY 09 procedure amended by FDC NOTAM: LNAV/VNAV MDA raised to 480ft, LPV DA raised to 340ft. These higher minimums override the printed chart. Mandatory compliance per FDC NOTAM authority.
required_safety_constraints: Poor braking action requires Part 121 landing distance calculation — if calculation exceeds available runway, alternate runway or divert required
CAT III to CAT II downgrade is mandatory — crews cannot plan CAT III operations on RWY 27
FDC NOTAM procedure amendment overrides chart minimums — higher minimums are legally mandatory
Combined braking action poor + CAT II limitation on primary runway creates a compound hazard
evidence_requirements: FAA AC 91-6A — Braking action poor performance

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. CYYZ=Toronto Pearson (Canadian ICAO identifier), KBOS=Boston Logan (KZBW FIR). CAT III to CAT II downgrade format per FAA JO 7930.2S ILS NOTAM. FDC NOTAM procedure amendment per FAA JO 7930.2S FDC format. Poor braking + CAT II creates compound approach hazard.

prompt:
Flight context: Piper Seminole, Part 91, VFR, KMRY (Monterey) to KSJC (San Jose), departure 0100 UTC (1700 PST), ETA 0130 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0781/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QOBCE/IV/NBO/A/000/012/3638N12134W010
A) KMRY B) 2601200000 C) 2601302359
E) OBST WIND TURBINES (ASN 2025-AWP-09034-OE) COMPLEX 2NM EAST ARPT ELEV 1340FT (1200FT AGL) LGTD.

NOTAM 2: A1234/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QRTCA/IV/NBO/W/000/030/3650N12200W020
A) KMRY B) 2601200000 C) 2601202359
E) TFR. AERIAL SURVEY OPERATIONS. 20NM RADIUS 3650N 12200W SFC TO 3000FT MSL.
gold_decision:
Two hazards for this night VFR departure:
1. Wind turbines 2NM east of KMRY at 1340ft MSL / 1200ft AGL, lighted: This is a CRITICAL hazard for a night VFR departure. The Piper Seminole has a service ceiling of approximately 12,000ft and typical initial climb well below 1340ft MSL. East departure from KMRY must maintain terrain and obstacle clearance. At 1340ft MSL / 1200ft AGL, the turbines are very tall obstacles in the Monterey area (local terrain ~140ft MSL). VFR pilot must have positive obstacle clearance before turning east.
2. TFR aerial survey 20NM radius near 3650N 12200W SFC to 3000ft MSL: Active 0000-2359 UTC (all day). The TFR is centered approximately 12NM north of KMRY. A direct KMRY-KSJC routing via HWY 1 / coast may transit this TFR. VFR flight in this corridor below 3000ft MSL is not authorized during the TFR. Must route around or above the TFR.
required_safety_constraints: Wind turbine at 1200ft AGL is a lethal hazard for a night VFR departure — must have terrain clearance plan before departing east
TFR covers direct route between KMRY and KSJC at low altitude — route must be checked against TFR boundary coordinates
Night VFR visibility must allow positive obstacle clearance — see-and-avoid is degraded at night
TFR violation can result in enforcement and military intercept
acceptable_variants: KSJC is approximately 35NM NNE of KMRY — agent noting that direct route does not necessarily enter the 20NM radius is acceptable if the geometry is correctly evaluated
evidence_requirements: KMRY (Monterey Peninsula Airport) terrain and elevation approximately 257ft MSL

provenance.generation_rule: Two NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KMRY=Monterey Peninsula Airport (~257ft MSL). Wind turbine ELEV 1340ft/1200ft AGL consistent with Salinas/Monterey area wind turbine heights. TFR center 3650N 12200W is approximately Monterey Bay area — routing geometry is intentionally ambiguous to test TFR boundary analysis. ASN number notional.

prompt:
Flight context: B787-9, Part 121, RJTT (Tokyo Haneda) to YSSY (Sydney), departure 0100 UTC, ETA 1100 UTC. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0512/26 NOTAMN
Q) RJJJ/QGPAS/IV/NBO/E/000/999/3500N14000E400
A) RJJJ B) 2601200000 C) 2601202359
E) GPS UNRELIABLE WESTERN JAPAN FIR. RNAV OPERATIONS DEGRADED.

NOTAM 2: A0781/26 NOTAMN
Q) YMMM/QRPCA/IV/M/E/000/450/2500S13500E100
A) YMMM B) 2601200600 C) 2601201800
E) MILITARY EXERCISE AREA ACT. DARWIN FIR. SFC TO FL450. COORDS 2000S 1300E, 2000S 1400E, 3000S 1400E, 3000S 1300E.

NOTAM 3: A1034/26 NOTAMN
Q) YSSS/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3357S15110E005
A) YSSY B) 2601200000 C) 2601212359
E) ILS RWY 34L GS U/S.
gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. GPS unreliable western Japan FIR all day: Departure from RJTT at 0100 UTC transits western Japan FIR (RJJJ). RNAV departure and en-route operations in this sector are degraded. IRS/INS navigation must be primary. Crews must have non-GPS navigation capability confirmed.
2. Military exercise Darwin FIR SFC-FL450, 0600-1800 UTC: Pacific routing from RJTT to YSSY may transit northern Australian airspace (YMMM Darwin FIR). Exercise area coordinates define a large block of airspace. B787 cruise altitude typically FL350-FL390 — within FL450 ceiling. ETA 1100 UTC is within exercise window. Routing must be checked against exercise coordinates and ATC clearance obtained.
3. YSSY ILS RWY 34L glideslope unserviceable: Primary arrival runway at YSSY affected. LOC-only approach to 34L available, or ILS 34R (if normal). Weather at arrival must be checked against LOC-only minimums.
required_safety_constraints: GPS unreliable in Japan FIR: IRS/INS is required navigation backup for entire departure segment
Military exercise FL450 ceiling is above typical B787 cruise — all altitudes including cruise are within restriction; must obtain ATC clearance or routing around the area
YSSY ILS 34L GS U/S: LOC-only minimums apply; must confirm weather at ETA against LOC-only minimums
Darwin FIR military exercise requires active coordination — not merely awareness
acceptable_variants: If B787 routes via eastern Pacific avoiding Darwin FIR, NOTAM 2 may not apply — agent should flag this routing dependency

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per ICAO Annex 15. RJTT=Tokyo Haneda, RJJJ=Japan Fukuoka FIR, YMMM=Darwin FIR (Australia), YSSY=Sydney Kingsford Smith, YSSS=Sydney TMA. Military exercise in Darwin FIR is a realistic scenario per Australia-US military training exercises. Darwin FIR coordinates are representative northern Australian airspace. GPS unreliable Japan FIR is documented occurrence.

prompt:

NOTAM 1: A0231/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZLA/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3646N11946W005
A) KFAT B) 2601202000 C) 2601210400
E) ILS RWY 29L LOC U/S.

gold_decision:
Three hazards:
1. KFAT ILS RWY 29L localizer unserviceable 2000-0400 UTC: ETA 2300 UTC is within the outage. ILS approach to RWY 29L is unavailable (LOC U/S means both full ILS and LOC-only are unavailable). Must use RNAV or VOR approach to RWY 29L, or alternate runway with working ILS.
2. KFAT ATIS frequency 124.0 U/S 2100-0300 UTC: ETA 2300 UTC within outage. Use alternate ATIS frequency 132.45 for pre-arrival weather, runway, and NOTAM information.
3. Crane obstacle 0.8NM south of KFAT at 342ft MSL / 120ft AGL, lighted: Active Jan 15-30. South departure or arrival path must account for this obstacle. RWY 29L approach from the east is not directly affected, but any circling approach or missed approach to the south must clear this obstacle.
required_safety_constraints: ILS LOC U/S eliminates the ILS AND the LOC-only approach — both require the localizer signal
ATIS frequency change is operationally critical for a night IFR arrival — must obtain current information before commencing approach
Crane 0.8NM south is within circling area for approaches to KFAT — circling minimums must provide obstacle clearance
Part 135 IFR operations require instrument approach procedure confirmation before departure (14 CFR §135.293)
evidence_requirements: FAA JO 7930.2S contractions (LOC, ATIS, OBST, LGTD, AGL)

prompt:

gold_decision:
Three items to assess:
1. TFR near KMIA departure area 1300-1700 UTC: Space launch TFR centered at 2548N 08013W (approximately Cape Canaveral/Kennedy Space Center area, ~150NM north of KMIA). 40NM radius, SFC-18000ft. Direct KMIA-TJSJ routing heads southeast, away from the Cape Canaveral area. Miami is approximately 25-30 degrees south of the TFR center. The TFR likely does not directly affect the KMIA-TJSJ eastbound departure track, but must be verified against the specific departure routing. Flag as a routing check item.
2. TJSJ RWY 08/26 closed 0900-1800 UTC: ETA 1615 UTC within window. Runway 08/26 is the primary east-west runway at TJSJ. With it closed, must use RWY 10/28 for landing. NOTAM 3 confirms ILS RWY 10 and RWY 28 are both normal.
3. TJSJ ILS RWY 10 and RWY 28 normal: Positive confirmation that approach capability to 10/28 is unaffected.
required_safety_constraints: TFR at Cape Canaveral area: verify that KMIA eastbound departure routing does not penetrate 40NM radius around 2548N 08013W
TJSJ RWY 08/26 closed forces landing on RWY 10 or 28 — performance data required for these runways
ILS RWY 10/28 confirmed normal — a positive NOTAM indicator (rare) that specifically clears the fallback approach options
Space launch TFRs are non-negotiable — any routing uncertainty requires ATC coordination
acceptable_variants: Correctly identifying that the TFR center is distant from the KMIA-TJSJ routing but must still be verified is the right nuanced answer
evidence_requirements: TFR spatial analysis: KMIA approximately 2538N 08017W; TFR center 2548N 08013W; 10NM separation
TJZS=San Juan, Puerto Rico ARTCC equivalent

provenance.generation_rule: Three NOTAMs per FAA JO 7930.2S. KMIA=Miami International (KZJX FIR), TJSJ=San Juan Luis Munoz Marin (TJZS=San Juan FIR). Space launch TFR near Cape Canaveral/KSC (2548N 08013W is approximately Kennedy Space Center latitude/longitude). KMIA at 2538N 08017W is approximately 10NM south of TFR center, making it a boundary case. TJSJ has two runway pairs: 08/26 (primary) and 10/28.
